purpose: Defines the controlled vocabulary of failure-pattern categories that a SimulationResult may reference. Results reference these category IDs; they never invent new failure categories inline. Categories cover hand, land, mana, ramp, and color failures for the committed Sprint 2 metrics.

includes: The closed set of failure-pattern category IDs and their meaning.
The metric family each category relates to.
The distinction between mana-quantity failures and color-access failures.
excludes: Observed frequencies (those belong to a SimulationResult).
Interpretation of why a pattern matters (that belongs to reasoning).
Gameplay, combat, or opponent failure modes.

category_id_rule: Every category_id is lowercase snake_case, unique, and stable across the taxonomy version. A SimulationResult failure pattern must reference exactly one category_id from this set.

categories.description: Opening or post-mulligan hand contained no lands and was rejected by the keep rule.
One-land hand that failed the modeled early-acceleration exception and was mulliganed.
Hand contained six or seven lands and was rejected by the keep rule.
Repeated mulligans reduced the final kept hand size via London bottoming.
A required land drop for the target turn was not made because no playable land was available.
Total available mana by the target turn fell below the amount the metric required.
No modeled ramp source was available (or accessible, per the metric variant) by the target turn.
One required commander color had no online source by the target turn while other colors were available.
Two or more required colors had no online source by the target turn.
At least one of the five colors was missing an online source by the target turn, so full five-color availability was not reached.
Sufficient total mana was available but the specific required colors were not, isolating a color-access failure from a mana-quantity failure.

A card in the modeled deck had behavior that is neither resolvable from canonical Card Facts nor declared in card_semantics.json; it was surfaced as a limitation and contributed nothing to any success metric.

family_summary.ramp: Access to or availability of acceleration.
family_summary.boundary: Evidence-boundary limitation, not a gameplay failure.

quantity_versus_color_distinction.statement: The taxonomy deliberately separates mana-quantity failures (missed_land_drop, insufficient_mana_by_turn, ramp_not_available_by_turn) from color-access failures (single_color_missing_by_turn, multiple_colors_missing_by_turn, five_color_not_complete_by_turn, mana_present_wrong_colors) so that a SimulationResult can distinguish 'not enough mana' from 'not the right colors'.

explicit_boundary.statement: This taxonomy defines category identities only. It records no frequencies, no results, and no interpretation, and it authorizes no deck change.
